role_title: Technical Program Manager, Data Center Infrastructure

seniority.level: Senior IC / Manager (likely Staff TPM or Engineering Manager equivalent)
level_signals: 7+ years in data center construction, operations, or infrastructure program management
Management experience building and leading TPM or program management teams
Comp range $365K-$435K base, consistent with senior/staff-level at Anthropic
Responsible for hiring and building out the TPM function

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 7+ years in data center construction, operations, or infrastructure program management at hyperscale or critical infrastructure companies
must_haves.qualifier: at hyperscale or critical infrastructure companies

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have management experience building and leading TPM or program management teams
Have led multi-site infrastructure programs with compressed timelines and complex cross-functional dependencies among multiple partners and vendors
Excel at aligning multiple partners, vendors, and internal engineering teams around shared deliverables
Can bridge physical infrastructure and software/security teams
Thrive in ambiguity and make decisions with incomplete information under schedule pressure
Communicate complex program status clearly to executives
Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience
Travel (20%) to sites to validate progress and build contractor relationships

scope.surface: Data center infrastructure programs — construction through commissioning and day-to-day operations across multiple global sites
scope.business_model: internal infrastructure / compute enablement for AI research and products
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of data center sites to production readiness
core_responsibilities: Manage and develop the DC infrastructure TPM team; hire and build out the function as we scale
Lead program reviews and executive communications on delivery status, risks, and blockers
Drive construction-to-production execution across multiple sites, coordinating with partners, contractors, and internal teams
Own overall schedule tracking, risk identification, and mitigation; creating clear visibility for leadership
Align with energy and location strategy and support site selection and due diligence criteria to facilitate scaling well in advance of aggressive compute demand
Manage long pole dependencies and long-lead time development & procurement across a dynamically changing global marketplace
Partner with DC and Compute teams to drive handover criteria between site completion, equipment deployment, and operations
Coordinate with Infrastructure, Security, and Compliance teams on evolving requirements; versioning, optimization, and expansion across sites
Drive partner accountability on contractual milestones and commercial commitments
Coordinate equipment delivery and manage vendor accountability on schedules; tracking production and shipping milestones while maintaining productive partnerships
Build repeatable processes and playbooks that scale across multiple concurrent site deployments
Travel (20%) to sites to validate progress and build contractor relationships

compensation.equity_form: optional equity donation matching mentioned (suggests options/RSUs are part of package)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($365K-$435K base)
Clear ownership scope — owning the DC infrastructure TPM function end-to-end
People management responsibility signals genuine leadership role, not just IC
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Applications accepted on a rolling basis with no hard deadline
red_flags: 20% travel required — may be a constraint for some candidates
Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco; no remote option stated
Role involves building and hiring a new function from scratch — high ambiguity and workload in early stages
Hard requirement for 7+ years specifically at hyperscale or critical infrastructure companies is narrow

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, people management responsibility, and years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from context as internal infrastructure enablement)
location.cities_listed (inferred as San Francisco from HQ reference; no explicit city named for this role)
ambiguities: The JD does not specify a city for the role beyond Anthropic's HQ in San Francisco. Location is inferred from the company headquarters reference.

No explicit nice-to-haves section exists in the JD; all qualifications listed under 'You may be a good fit if you' were treated as must-haves given unconditional framing. The education credential is listed in the logistics section and treated as soft per Anthropic's standard language.
The role involves both IC program management and managing/hiring a TPM team — it is simultaneously a senior IC and people manager role, which is slightly ambiguous for leveling.
